content:
3 min read
Just now

--

CSP_NONCE only works correctly when both the backend and the Angular frontend cooperate. Neither side alone is sufficient.
Here’s why.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Angular_CSP_NONCE_How_It_WorksPress enter or click to view image in full size

CSP_NONCE implementation guide with Angular & Node.jsBackend’s responsibility
The backend is responsible for:

• Generating a cryptographically secure random nonce for every HTTP request.

• Sending the CSP header containing that nonce.

• Making the nonce available to the Angular application (for example, via a <meta> tag or a bootstrap script).
Example:
Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' 'nonce-r4nd0m123';
style-src 'self' 'nonce-r4nd0m123';And in the HTML:
<meta name="csp-nonce" content="r4nd0m123">Without the backend, there is no trusted nonce for the browser to validate.
Frontend (Angular)’s responsibility
Angular reads the nonce and provides it using the CSP_NONCE injection token.
Example:
import { bootstrapApplication, CSP_NONCE } from '@angular/core';bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, {
providers: [
{
provide: CSP_NONCE,
useValue: document
.querySelector('meta[name="csp-nonce"]')
?.getAttribute('content')
}
]
});
Now, whenever Angular creates a runtime <style> element, it generates:
<style nonce="r4nd0m123">instead of:
<style>How they work together
Request
Browser ---------------------> Backend Generate nonce
r4nd0m123
CSP Header
style-src 'nonce-r4nd0m123'
HTML
<meta name="csp-nonce"
content="r4nd0m123">
Browser <--------------------- Response
Angular starts
Reads nonce
provide(CSP_NONCE)
Angular creates
<style nonce="r4nd0m123">
Browser verifies
Header nonce
==
Style nonce
✔ Allow
What if only the backend implements it?
The backend sends:
style-src 'nonce-abc123'But Angular creates:
<style>The browser compares:
Expected nonce: abc123
Received: noneResult:
❌ BlockedYou’ll typically see a browser console error like:
Refused to apply inline style because it violates
Content Security Policy.What if only Angular uses CSP_NONCE?
Suppose Angular does:
provide: CSP_NONCE,
useValue: "abc123"but the backend sends:
Content-Security-Policy:
style-src 'self';or sends a different nonce.
Then Angular produces:
<style nonce="abc123">The browser ignores the nonce because the CSP header doesn’t authorize it.
Again:
❌ BlockedDo SPAs always need backend changes?
It depends on how your app is served.
Scenario 1: Angular + Node/Java/.NET backend (recommended)
Angular
│
▼
Node / Spring / .NETBackend generates nonce
✅ Use both backend and frontend.
Scenario 2: Angular served by Nginx
Browser
│
▼
Nginx
│
AngularIf Nginx serves index.html, then Nginx (or another reverse proxy in front of it) must generate the nonce and inject it into both the CSP header and the HTML. Static files alone cannot produce a fresh random nonce per request.
Scenario 3: Pure static hosting (e.g., simple static file hosting)
Angular filesIf there’s no server capable of generating a per-request nonce, you generally cannot implement nonce-based CSP correctly. In that case, teams often use other CSP strategies (such as hashes for known inline content or avoiding inline content entirely), though those approaches have different trade-offs.
Summary
| Component | Responsibility |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Backend / Web server | Generate a new random nonce for every request |
| Backend / Web server | Send the nonce in the `Content-Security-Policy` header |
| Backend / Web server | Expose the same nonce to the Angular app (e.g., via a `<meta>` tag) |
| Angular frontend | Read the nonce and provide it through `CSP_NONCE` |
| Angular runtime | Add the nonce to runtime-generated `<style>` elements |CSP_NONCE is a coordinated security mechanism. The backend (or the server serving your app) establishes the browser's trust policy by issuing the nonce, and the Angular frontend uses that same nonce on the runtime-generated content. If either side is missing or the nonces don't match, the browser will block the protected inline content.
